column1: Edit Story ID, Persona, Epic, Priority, Story, and Acceptance Criteria columns directly. Status and Notes are yours to manage.

column5: User Story
column6: Acceptance Criteria
column7: Mobile Note

column5: As a student, I want to browse competitions by category and grade level so that I can quickly find relevant ones without scrolling through irrelevant results.
column6:
1. Category filter tabs: Academic, Arts, Sports, Debate
2. Grade filter: SD / SMP / SMA
3. Filtered results update without full page reload
4. Each card shows: name, organizer, deadline, fee, category badge
column7: Filters persist across sessions; filter chips visible above fold on 360px screens

column5: As a student, I want to view full competition details so that I can decide whether to register before committing.
column6:
1. Detail page shows: name, organizer, deadline, fee, competition date, format, location, required docs, eligibility
2. Register CTA is above the fold on mobile
3. If quota full or closed, CTA replaced with disabled state + explanation
column7: Renders fully on 360px without horizontal scroll; skeleton loader within 300ms

column5: As a student, I want to create my profile once so that my details auto-fill into every future registration.
column6:
1. Fields: full name, date of birth, NISN, school, grade, phone, photo
2. Profile auto-fills all future registration forms
3. Edits do not affect past submitted registrations
4. All mandatory fields validated before saving
column7: Phone verified via OTP on sign-up; camera permission requested only for photo upload

column5: As a student, I want to upload documents once and reuse them across registrations so that I never repeat the same upload.
column6:
1. Supported: JPG, PNG, PDF up to 10MB
2. Docs labelled by type: student ID, report card, recommendation letter
3. One-tap attachment during registration from stored vault
4. Docs encrypted at rest; accessible only by student and target organizer
column7: Camera capture + gallery import; compressed to <2MB client-side before upload

column5: As a student, I want to complete a competition registration in under 3 minutes so that the process feels as easy as online shopping.
column6:
1. Form pre-filled from profile (name, school, grade, NISN)
2. Required docs shown as checklist; student selects from vault
3. Review screen shown before submission
4. Unique registration number issued on submit
5. Confirmation sent via push notification and WhatsApp
column7: Single-column layout; each step has a progress indicator; no back-navigation mid-payment

column5: As a student, I want to pay using GoPay, OVO, or bank transfer so that I can use my preferred local payment method.
column6:
1. Payment options: GoPay, OVO, Dana, BCA VA, Mandiri VA
2. GoPay/OVO deep-links into wallet app and returns automatically on success
3. Bank transfer shows virtual account number with 24-hour expiry
4. Payment status updates automatically — no manual confirmation
5. Receipt PDF available after payment
column7: Deep-link return works on iOS and Android; falls back to in-app browser if deep-link fails

column5: As a student, I want to track all my registrations in one dashboard so that I always know what is pending, paid, or confirmed.
column6:
1. Statuses: Draft / Submitted / Paid / Confirmed / Rejected
2. Tapping a registration shows full detail and receipt
3. Rejected entries show the organizer reason
4. Default sort: soonest deadline first
column7: Color-coded status badges; pull-to-refresh supported; renders on 360px

column5: As a student, I want push notification reminders before registration deadlines so that I never miss the cut-off.
column6:
1. Notification at 9am local time, 3 days before deadline
2. Second notification at 9am local time, 1 day before deadline
3. Only sent if registration is not yet completed
4. Tapping notification deep-links to that competition's registration screen
5. User can disable per-competition reminders in settings
column7: Works when app is backgrounded; fallback to in-app inbox if push permission denied

column5: As a parent, I want to link my account to my child's profile so that I can monitor registrations and pay on their behalf.
column6:
1. Link request sent using child's phone number
2. Child must approve the link
3. Parent sees read-only view of child's registration dashboard
4. Parent can initiate and complete payment for pending registrations
5. One parent account supports multiple children
column7: Link request sent via WA/SMS if child is not in app; approval requires child confirmation

column5: As a parent, I want WhatsApp updates on my child's registration status so that I stay informed without opening the app.
column6:
1. WA message on: registration confirmed, payment received, organizer approval/rejection
2. Message includes competition name, child name, and deep-link to app
3. Parent can opt out of WA notifications in settings
4. Uses WhatsApp Business API pre-approved template messages
column7: WA deep-link opens app if installed; falls back to web view

column5: As a teacher, I want to register multiple students for the same competition in one session so that I do not repeat the process individually.
column6:
1. Select multiple students from school roster in one screen
2. Shared fields filled once and applied to all
3. Student docs pulled automatically from each vault
4. Payment can be batched or split per student
5. Summary screen before confirming bulk submission
column7: Optimized for tablet (768px+); mobile supports up to 10 students at once

column5: As a school admin, I want a dashboard of all students' registrations so that I have a full audit trail.
column6:
1. Shows: student name, competition, status, payment status, submission date
2. Filterable by competition, status, and grade
3. Exportable to CSV
4. Admin can view but not modify individual registrations
column7: Table view on desktop/tablet; card list on mobile

column5: As an organizer, I want to create and publish a competition listing so that students can discover and register through the platform.
column6:
1. Required fields: name, category, education level, reg open/close dates, competition date, entry fee, quota, required docs, eligibility
2. Save as draft before publishing
3. Published listing appears in student feed within 15 minutes
4. Organizer can edit listing after publishing without affecting existing registrations
5. Must accept platform terms before first listing
column7: Web portal only; mobile-responsive

column5: As an organizer, I want to review and approve or reject registrations so that I can control who participates.
column6:
1. Dashboard lists all registrations with status and submitted documents
2. Approve or reject with mandatory reason for rejection
3. Rejection reason auto-communicated to student via push + WA
4. Bulk-approve all paid and complete registrations
5. Confirmed registrations are locked for the student
column7: Web portal only

column5: As an organizer, I want to export a confirmed participant list so that I can use it for logistics.
column6:
1. Export as CSV and PDF
2. Fields: student name, NISN, school, grade, category, reg date, payment status
3. Filtered to confirmed only by default
4. Available once at least 1 registration is confirmed
column7: Web portal only

column5: As a representative, I want to register as a regional partner so that I can be authorized to promote competitions and manage schools in my area.
column6:
1. Submit application: name, organization, region/country, contact
2. Platform admin reviews and approves/rejects
3. Approved rep receives partner dashboard access
4. Rep assigned a region scope limiting visibility
5. Rep receives unique partner code for attribution tracking
column7: Application form web-based; dashboard mobile-responsive

column5: As a representative, I want to manage school accounts in my region so that I can onboard schools and keep their information up to date.
column6:
1. Add, edit, deactivate school accounts within assigned region
2. School admin receives invite email/WA to join under rep's region
3. Rep sees list of all managed schools with account status
4. Rep cannot access or modify student personal data
column7: School management is web portal only

column5: As a representative, I want to collect and forward registrations from schools in my region so that I act as the verified regional intermediary.
column6:
1. Rep dashboard shows all registrations from schools in their region
2. Rep can mark a batch as 'forwarded to organizer'
3. Organizer sees forwarding status in participant list
4. Rep cannot modify registration content — view and forward only
5. Audit log tracks every forwarding action
column7: Web portal only; mobile-responsive for status checking

column5: As a representative, I want to handle regional payment collection so that schools in my area can pay through me rather than individually.
column6:
1. Rep generates consolidated payment invoice per competition per region
2. Schools pay via platform-generated virtual account
3. Rep confirms receipt; platform marks registrations as paid
4. Rep payout processed after competition registration closes
5. All transactions logged with rep ID for audit
column7: Invoice generation web portal only; payment status visible on mobile

column5: As a representative, I want to share competition listings with schools in my region so that I can actively promote events I partner on.
column6:
1. Rep generates shareable link or WA-ready message for any published competition
2. Shared link includes rep's partner code for attribution
3. Rep dashboard shows click and registration counts from their shares
4. Rep cannot create or edit competition listings — promotion only
column7: Share to WA is primary action; deep-link routes student to competition detail page

column5: As a student, I want to see competition recommendations based on my registration history so that I discover relevant competitions I might have missed.
column6:
1. A "Recommended for you" section appears at the top of the Discover tab after a student's first registration
2. Recommendations are filtered to match the student's grade level — no out-of-grade competitions shown
3. Already-registered competitions are never shown in recommendations
4. Expired or closed competitions are never shown
5. If fewer than 3 recommendations exist, section is hidden — not shown with empty or irrelevant results
6. Student can tap "Not interested" on any recommendation card to remove it permanently
column7: "Recommended for you" section hidden until student has at least 1 completed registration. Renders above the fold on 360px screens.

column5: As a student, I want to receive a push notification after registering for a competition that shows similar competitions so that I can keep exploring without having to search.
column6:
1. Push notification sent 30 minutes after payment confirmed (not on submission — only after paid)
2. Notification includes the name of 1 recommended competition and its deadline
3. Tapping the notification deep-links directly to that competition's detail page
4. If push permission is denied, in-app banner shown instead on next app open
5. Max 1 post-registration recommendation push per day — not one per registration if multiple are done
column7: Push respects global 2/day cap and 10pm–8am silent hours. In-app banner fallback if push denied.

column5: As a student, I want to be notified when a new competition matching my interests is published so that I never miss a registration window.
column6:
1. Alert sent within 1 hour of an organizer publishing a new competition
2. Alert only sent if competition matches student's grade AND at least 1 past registration category
3. Maximum 1 new competition push notification per day — batch multiple matches into one message
4. Student can disable "new competition" alerts separately from other notification types in settings
5. Same competition is never sent twice to the same student
column7: Match threshold: grade match + ≥1 category match required. Batched into 1 push/day maximum.

column5: As a student, I want to receive a reminder about competitions I viewed but did not register for when the deadline is approaching so that I don't miss competitions I was interested in.
column6:
1. Reminder fires if student viewed competition detail for ≥10 seconds and has NOT registered
2. First reminder: 3 days before registration deadline at 9am local time
3. Second reminder: 1 day before deadline at 9am local time
4. Reminders stop immediately if student registers or taps "Not interested"
5. Max 2 reminders per competition per student — never more
6. Delivered via push first; WhatsApp fallback if push is denied or undelivered
column7: View duration tracked in analytics (≥10s threshold). Push first, WA fallback. Silent hours enforced.

column5: As a student, I want to receive a weekly summary of competitions matching my interests via WhatsApp or email so that I stay informed without opening the app every day.
column6:
1. Digest sent every Sunday at 9am local time
2. Content: top 5 matched competitions + up to 2 closing within 7 days (deadline urgency)
3. WA message uses pre-approved Meta template — plain text with competition names, deadlines, and one deep-link
4. Email uses HTML template with competition cards, fee badges, and individual Register CTAs
5. Student can opt out of digest independently — does not affect transactional notifications
6. Digest skipped if fewer than 3 matching competitions exist that week
7. Email must include unsubscribe link (legal requirement — UU PDP)
column7: WA template requires Meta pre-approval (1–3 days). Email needs unsubscribe link. Digest counts toward 2 WA/week cap.

column5: As a student, I want an in-app notification centre where I can see all past announcements and recommendations so that I never miss anything even if I dismissed a push notification.
column6:
1. Notification centre accessible from a bell icon in the app header on all main screens
2. Shows all notifications: recommendations, announcements, registration updates — sorted newest first
3. Unread notifications show a badge count on the bell icon (max display: 99+)
4. Tapping any notification item navigates to the relevant competition or screen
5. Notifications older than 30 days are automatically archived (not deleted)
6. Student can mark all as read, or delete individual notifications
column7: Bell icon + badge visible on all 3 main tabs. Serves as fallback inbox when push permission is denied.

column5: As a student, I want granular control over which notifications I receive and on which channels so that I can stay informed without feeling spammed.
column6:
1. Settings screen shows separate toggles for: New competition alerts / Post-registration recommendations / Deadline reminders / Weekly digest / Platform announcements
2. Each type has per-channel sub-toggle: Push / WhatsApp / Email
3. Transactional notifications (registration confirmed, payment received) cannot be turned off — only delivery channel can change
4. Student can set a "quiet hours" window — no push notifications sent during that time
5. Preferences saved server-side — persist across devices and app reinstalls
column7: Quiet hours default: 10pm–8am. Transactional notifications always delivered regardless of preferences.
